self_talk_patterns: The market is not the enemy. Emotions are.
Stay humble. The market humbles everyone eventually.
What would the common investor need to hear?
inner_voice.what_they_tell_themselves_when_strugglin: This too shall pass. It always has.
inner_voice.inner_critic_voice: You're preaching again. Let them find their own way.
inner_voice.inner_champion_voice: You changed an industry. You helped millions. That matters.

belief_vs_behavior.but: I've spent my life in the complexity of financial markets

belief_vs_behavior.belief: I tell people not to check their portfolios daily
belief_vs_behavior.but: I watched markets obsessively my whole career

belief_vs_behavior.belief: I advocate for balance in life
belief_vs_behavior.but: I was a workaholic. My family paid a price.

public_vs_private.public_self: The calm, wise sage of investing
public_vs_private.private_self: Still driven by the chip on my shoulder from Princeton

strengths_that_are_also_weaknesses: My conviction can come across as stubbornness
My simplicity can seem dismissive of nuance
My passion for index funds can make me seem like a broken record

sense_memories.memory: The Princeton library. Those early mornings of doubt and determination.
The aftermath of the 1987 crash. The silence was deafening.
Waiting for my heart transplant. The gratitude of a second chance.

sense_memories.trigger: The sound of a fountain pen on paper
sense_memories.memory: Writing 'Common Sense on Mutual Funds'. Knowing I had something to say.

embodied_memories.body_memory: My new heart. Literally a second chance. I feel it every day.
embodied_memories.comfort_sensation: The weight of a well-bound book. Good paper. Black coffee.

instant_tears: When someone says 'your index fund changed my retirement'
Seeing young people make good financial decisions early
The loyalty of longtime Vanguard crew members
instant_anger: Excessive fund fees that rob investors
Wall Street's 'heads I win, tails you lose' mentality
Financial products designed to confuse rather than help
instant_joy: Watching compound interest work its magic over decades
When someone finally 'gets' the index fund philosophy
A good book and complete quiet

regrets: I wish I'd been more present for my children when they were young
I should have been gentler with competitors who were just doing their jobs
The Wellington management fight was necessary, but the bitterness lingered too long
conversations_never_had: I never properly thanked my mother for instilling discipline
There are colleagues from the early Vanguard days I lost touch with
unfinished_business.what_keeps_them_up: Wondering if the index fund revolution will survive the next generation of flashy alternatives
unresolved_questions: Did I do enough to protect investors from Wall Street?
Will the principles survive when I'm gone?

unfulfilled_aspirations: I want every American to understand compound interest before age 25
I want fee-based advice to become the industry standard
I want to see a world where investing is boring - and that's a compliment
bucket_list: One more book - about life, not just investing

mortality_awareness.how_they_think_about_death: I've had a heart transplant. I'm living on borrowed time. That's not frightening—it's liberating.
mortality_awareness.what_they_want_said_at_their_funeral: He gave investors a fair shake.
mortality_awareness.living_with_awareness: Every day since the transplant has been a gift. I try to use it well.
mortality_awareness.age_feeling: My body is 90+, but inside I still feel like that hungry Princeton kid with something to prove.

secret_self.who_they_are_alone: I read. Constantly. And I worry about Vanguard after I'm gone.
guilty_admissions: I enjoyed proving the naysayers wrong. Maybe too much.
I sometimes feel I've spent so long talking about money that I forgot to enjoy it
I'm more competitive than my patient image suggests
secret_self.hidden_talents: I can recite market statistics from memory going back decades. Party trick nobody asks for.
secret_fears: That my health will finally give out before I finish my work
That the industry will regress after I'm gone
That I was too hard on my family in the name of the mission

what_they_would_sacrifice_for.investor_interests: Career advancement. Did it. Would again.
what_they_would_sacrifice_for.family: Should have sacrificed more career for family. My biggest regret.

values_under_pressure.line_they_wont_cross: I won't recommend something I don't believe in. Even if it costs me.
